narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: If we “just stop oil” like climate protesters want, six BILLION people could die
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The opening statement directly questions the effectiveness and predicts catastrophic human consequences of the climate policy advocated by the Just Stop Oil movement, framing it as genocidal.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The answer, in part, is propaganda. As the end of the biblical age of grace approaches, the world is increasingly coming unhinged, including environmentally as the birth pangs intensify. The theory that climate fanatics have adopted...
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text labels climate activists as 'fanatics' who have adopted a flawed theory due to propaganda, challenging their motives and intellectual integrity by suggesting they are deluded or acting on religious-like fervor.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: ...even though the environment itself, i.e., volcanoes, is emitting far more 'greenhouse gases' like carbon dioxide than the tailpipes on cars or the flatulence from cattle.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote minimizes the significance of human-caused emissions by claiming natural sources like volcanoes emit far more, thereby downplaying the human impact on climate change.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: (Related: According to Sultan Al Jaber, the president of COP28, there is 'no science' to back the 'green' phase-out of earth-based 'fossil' fuel use.)
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This parenthetical statement directly raises doubts about the scientific basis for phasing out fossil fuels, aligning with the narrative of questioning climate science and its policy recommendations.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Millions upon millions would die in the first few weeks, followed by billions more as the rest of the world and its oil- and gas-based systems collapse.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text vividly amplifies fears about the consequences of implementing climate policies, predicting immediate, apocalyptic societal collapse and mass death on a global scale.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: If we “just stop oil” like climate protesters want, six BILLION people could die
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The opening statement directly questions the effectiveness and catastrophic consequences of the climate policy goal advocated by protesters, framing it as genocidal.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: As the end of the biblical age of grace approaches, the world is increasingly coming unhinged, including environmentally as the birth pangs intensify. The theory that climate fanatics have adopted as to why this is all happening blames human activity and industry
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote frames the climate movement as 'fanatics' driven by a flawed 'theory' and connects their beliefs to apocalyptic, quasi-religious thinking, challenging their motives and intellectual integrity.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: the environment itself, i.e., volcanoes, is emitting far more 'greenhouse gases' like carbon dioxide than the tailpipes on cars or the flatulence from cattle.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement minimizes the significance of human-caused climate change by arguing that natural sources are a larger contributor, thereby downplaying the impact of anthropogenic emissions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: (Related: According to Sultan Al Jaber, the president of COP28, there is 'no science' to back the 'green' phase-out of earth-based 'fossil' fuel use.)
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This parenthetical statement, presented as a related fact, directly raises doubts about the scientific basis for phasing out fossil fuels, challenging the scientific consensus.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: This is just in the UK, mind you. Multiply these effects all around the world and you can see how quickly life as we currently know it would end almost instantly. Millions upon millions would die in the first few weeks, followed by billions more as the rest of the world and its oil- and gas-based systems collapse.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This passage explicitly amplifies fears about the consequences of implementing climate policies, predicting instant societal collapse and mass death on a global scale.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: If we “just stop oil” like climate protesters want, six BILLION people could die
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text directly criticizes the policy goal of stopping oil use by claiming it would lead to catastrophic death, questioning its effectiveness and highlighting perceived extreme negative consequences.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: As the end of the biblical age of grace approaches, the world is increasingly coming unhinged, including environmentally as the birth pangs intensify. The theory that climate fanatics have adopted as to why this is all happening blames human activity and industry
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text labels climate activists as 'fanatics' and frames their scientific theory as a misguided belief adopted during a perceived apocalyptic time, challenging their motives and integrity.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement minimizes the significance of human-caused climate change by arguing that natural sources are a larger contributor, thereby downplaying the impact of human activity.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The theory that climate fanatics have adopted as to why this is all happening blames human activity and industry... Even so, member of groups like Just Stop Oil have been deluded – or perhaps deluded themselves – into believing that they must appease the climate gods
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text challenges the motives and intellectual integrity of the climate movement, labeling them as 'fanatics' and 'deluded,' and characterizing their beliefs as akin to religious appeasement rather than science.
